question_text: A student has two cubes made of different materials. Cube X has sides of 5 cm and a mass of 625 g. Cube Y has sides of 4 cm and a mass of 256 g. Calculate the density of each cube and determine which material is denser.
correct_answer: Cube X: 5 g/cm³; Cube Y: 4 g/cm³; Cube X is denser
explanation: For Cube X: volume = 5³ = 125 cm³, so density = 625 ÷ 125 = 5 g/cm³. For Cube Y: volume = 4³ = 64 cm³, so density = 256 ÷ 64 = 4 g/cm³. Comparing the densities shows Cube X (5 g/cm³) is denser than Cube Y (4 g/cm³).
